title: tangy cheese-pull Ukrainian porcini Bao
description: A quick-fix, ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly Ukrainian dish featuring porcini and a tangy cheese pull. Serve in a soft, fluffy bao bun.
instructions: Combine yeast, sugar, and warm water to activate yeast.
Add flour to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and cut into 4-6 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten slightly into disks.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Cook the bao for 2-3 minutes per side, until golden brown.
Meanwhile, sauté sliced porcini mushrooms in the skillet with infuse-oil until tender.
Assemble the bao by placing a spoonful of sautéed porcini on each disk, then top with a spoonful of shredded cheddar cheese.
Place the bao under the broiler for 30-60 seconds, until cheese is melted and bubbly.
